rationale: Both correctly diagnose the run-level/dummy-weight issue and reach the same 'closed as invalid' disposition matching the thread, with the same fix (--run-level=full_model). Y is more thoroughly grounded: it cites more specific files/lines (runtime.py, assertions.py, stage_config.py ranges for both dummy and real load paths), explicitly ties in PR #4354 which the ground-truth maintainer comment references, and clarifies the subtle point that the full_model pytest marker is only a selection filter and does not itself set --run-level — a nuance X omits. Y also edges out on completeness by adding practical run requirements (weights cached, video path must exist, GPU memory split) and a constructive follow-up suggestion (warn/skip when a full_model-marked test collects at core_model) that speaks to preventing recurrence, whereas X's answer, while clean and correct, is comparatively thinner on citation depth and misses the #4354 linkage.
